Definition of “Secretary”, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–328, div. DD, § 1136 Stat. 5574Dec. 29, 2022“In this division [see Tables for classification], the term ‘Secretary’ means the Secretary of the Interior.”

children.label: i
children.text: surveys;

children.label: ii
children.text: maps;

children.label: iii
children.text: charts;

children.label: iv
children.text: satellite and airborne remote sensing data;

children.label: v
children.text: images; and

children.label: I
children.text: a surveyor;

children.label: II
children.text: a photogrammetrist;

children.label: III
children.text: a hydrographer;

children.label: IV
children.text: a geodesist; or

children.label: V
children.text: a cartographer.

children.label: i
children.text: a reference frame consisting of a current geodetic network that is consistent with, and not duplicative of, the National Geodic Survey of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration;

children.label: ii
children.text: a series of current and accurate large-scale maps;

children.label: iii
children.text: an existing cadastral boundary overlay delineating all cadastral parcels;

children.label: iv
children.text: a system for indexing and identifying each cadastral parcel; and

children.label: v
children.text: a series of land data files, each including the parcel identifier, which can be used to retrieve information and cross-reference between and among other existing data files that may contain information about the use, assets, and infrastructure of each parcel.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “Federal real property” means any real property owned, leased, or otherwise managed by the Secretary concerned.

children.label: i
children.text: real property held in trust by the Federal Government for the benefit of 1 or more Indian Tribes or individual Indians; or

children.label: ii
children.text: restricted land owned by an Indian Tribe or individual Indians.

children.label: A
children.text: land;

children.label: B
children.text: buildings, crops, forests, or other resources still attached to or within the land;

children.label: C
children.text: improvements or fixtures permanently attached to the land;

children.label: D
children.text: any structure on the land; or

children.label: E
children.text: any interest, benefit, right, or privilege in the property described in subparagraphs (A) through (D).

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service.

children.heading: Cadastre of Federal real property
children.label: b Cadastre of Federal real property

children.heading: Interagency data standardization
children.label: 1 Interagency data standardization
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , the Secretaries concerned shall jointly develop and adopt interagency standards to ensure compatibility and interoperability among applicable Federal databases with respect to the collection and dissemination of data relating to Federal real property.December 29, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: resource development and conservation;

children.label: B
children.text: agricultural use;

children.label: C
children.text: active forest management;

children.label: D
children.text: environmental protection; and

children.label: E
children.text: other use of the real property.

children.label: i
children.text: the statutory authorization for each existing real property inventory or component of a cadastre; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount expended by the Federal Government for each existing real property inventory or component of a cadastre in fiscal year 2022;

children.label: B
children.text: the existing real property inventories or any components of any cadastre of Federal real property currently authorized by law or maintained by the Secretary concerned that will be eliminated or consolidated into the multipurpose cadastre under paragraph (2);

children.label: i
children.text: the existing real property inventories or any components of any cadastre of Federal real property currently authorized by law or maintained by the Secretary concerned that will not be eliminated or consolidated into the multipurpose cadastre under paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: a justification for not eliminating or consolidating an existing real property inventory or component of a cadastre described in clause (i) into the multipurpose cadastre under paragraph (2);

children.label: D
children.text: the use of existing real property inventories or any components of any cadastre currently maintained by any unit of State or local government that can be used to identify Federal real property within that unit of government;

children.label: E
children.text: the cost savings that will be achieved by eliminating or consolidating duplicative or unneeded real property inventories or any components of any cadastre of Federal real property currently authorized by law or maintained by the Secretary concerned that will become part of the multipurpose cadastre under paragraph (2);

children.label: F
children.text: a plan for the implementation of this section, including a cost estimate and an assessment of the feasibility of using revenue from any transactional activity authorized by law to offset any costs of implementing this section; and

children.label: G
children.text: recommendations for any legislation necessary to increase the cost savings and enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of replacing, eliminating, or consolidating Federal real property inventories or any components of any cadastre of Federal real property currently authorized by law or maintained by the Secretary concerned.

children.label: i
children.text: participate (in accordance with section 216 of the E-Government Act of 2002 ( note; ) and section 757 of the Geospatial Data Act of 2018 ()) in the establishment of such standards and common protocols as are necessary to ensure the interoperability of geospatial information pertaining to the cadastre under paragraph (2) for all users of the information;44 U.S.C. 3501Public Law 107–34743 U.S.C. 2806

children.label: I
children.text: the cadastre under paragraph (2); and

children.label: II
children.text: any aspect of the development of the cadastre under paragraph (2);

children.label: iii
children.text: integrate, or make the cadastre interoperable with, the Federal Real Property Profile or other inventories established pursuant to Executive Order 13327 ( note; relating to Federal real property asset management), the Federal Assets Sale and Transfer Act of 2016 ( note; ), or the Federal Property Management Reform Act of 2016 (; ); and40 U.S.C. 12140 U.S.C. 1303Public Law 114–287Public Law 114–318130 Stat. 1608

children.label: iv
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, integrate with and leverage current cadastre activities of units of State and local government.

children.heading: Contracts considered surveying and mapping
children.label: B Contracts considered surveying and mapping

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A contract between the Secretaries concerned and a member of the private sector to provide products and services for the development of the cadastre shall be considered to be a contract for services of surveying and mapping (within the meaning of chapter 11 of title 40).

children.heading: Selection procedures
children.label: ii Selection procedures
children.text: A contract described in clause (i) shall be entered into in accordance with the selection procedures in chapter 11 of title 40.

children.label: A
children.text: in a graphically geo-enabled and searchable format; and

children.label: B
children.text: in a manner that is consistent with, and meets any requirements for integration with, the GeoPlatform established under section 758(a) of that Act ();43 U.S.C. 2807(a)

ensure that the inventory referred to in subsection (b) includes the identification of all land suitable for disposal and the appraised value of the land, if an appraisal has been conducted, in accordance with the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.); and43 U.S.C. 1701

in consultation with the Secretary of Defense and the Secretary of Homeland Security, prevent the disclosure of any parcel or parcels of land, any buildings or facilities on the land, or any information related to the land, buildings, or facilities if that disclosure would impair or jeopardize the national security or homeland defense of the United States.
considered to be geospatial data for purposes of the Geospatial Data Act of 2018 ( et seq.); and43 U.S.C. 2801
subject to the requirements of that Act.
creates any substantive or procedural right or benefit; or

children.label: A
children.text: any new surveying or mapping of Federal real property;

children.label: B
children.text: the evaluation of any parcel of land or other real property for potential management by a non-Federal entity;

children.label: C
children.text: the disposal of any Federal real property; or

children.label: i
children.text: the value of any parcel of Federal land or other real property; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the cultural and archaeological resources on any parcel of Federal land or other real property.
